observations: SCHEMA VALIDITY: `python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/contracts/034.json')), json.load(open('schema/contract.schema.json')))"` from the repository root -- exits 0, no error.

18-ENTRY LIVE BASELINE RE-RUN (all 18 commands extracted programmatically from the live contract's 'verification' array via json.load -- never retyped -- and executed from the repository root against the current, unmodified pre-implementation tree): entries 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 (file-existence, frontmatter, section-anchored content, and README-flip checks) correctly FAIL as expected pre-implementation (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md is absent and ecosystem/README.md's 02 row is still 'Planned'); entries 2, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18 (the anti-vacuity master proof and all non-goal/guard/regression checks) correctly PASS against the clean, unimplemented tree. This matches design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof's claimed pass/fail pattern exactly, independently re-verified rather than trusted from the generator's own claim.

ROUND-0 BACKSLASH-BACKTICK DEFECT GENUINELY FIXED, CHARACTER-EXACT: extracted verification entry 9's command string via json.load (not visual inspection) and confirmed byte-for-byte -- `grep -Fq '| 02 | \`02_evidence_baseline.md\` | Baseline | Shipped |' ecosystem/README.md` -- contains plain backticks with NO backslash character anywhere in the pattern. Re-ran the exact, unmodified entry-9 command inside a disposable local `git clone` of this repository (a fully separate git working tree under the evaluator's own scratchpad, never the real repository, per the probe-isolation rule) with `sed` used to genuinely flip only the 02 row's trailing cell from 'Planned' to 'Shipped': entry 9 PASSes (exit 0) against the corrected working tree, and entry 9 correctly FAILs (exit 1, 'ecosystem/README.md 02 row not flipped to Shipped') against the unmodified clone -- reproducing, character-exactly, the fix the round-0 validator rejection demanded, with the evaluator's own independent probe rather than a re-read of the generator's revision-1 claim.

ENTRY 9 + ENTRY 10 JOINTLY DISCRIMINATE THE WRONG-ROW AND MULTI-LINE GAMING VECTORS THE TASK SPECIFICALLY FLAGGED, PROVEN IN A DISPOSABLE CLONE (never the real repo): (a) correct flip -- only the 02 row's last cell changed Planned->Shipped -- entries 9 and 10 BOTH PASS (numstat exactly '1\t1\tecosystem/README.md'). (b) WRONG-ROW flip -- the 00 row's cell flipped to Shipped instead, 02 row left untouched -- entry 9 correctly FAILs ('ecosystem/README.md 02 row not flipped to Shipped', exit 1) even though entry 10 alone would PASS (the diff is still exactly one line), so the pair jointly rejects this gaming attempt; entry 9 is the entry that catches it, precisely as the task asked to prove. (c) TWO-LINE flip -- both the 00 row AND the 02 row flipped to Shipped -- entry 9 PASSes (02 row content is genuinely correct) but entry 10 correctly FAILs (numstat '2\t2\tecosystem/README.md', not '1\t1'), catching the extra out-of-scope edit entry 9 alone cannot see. No single-line or two-line mutation of the README table was found that passes both entries 9 and 10 simultaneously without the 02 row being the sole, correct edit.

SECTION-ANCHORED CONTENT CHECKS (entries 5-8) INDEPENDENTLY FIXTURE-PROVEN NON-VACUOUS AND MUTATION-DISCRIMINATING, using an evaluator-built GOOD fixture matching the contract's own planned_document_structure verbatim plus eight independently-derived BAD mutations, run via the exact, unmodified verification commands (no path-substitution artifacts) inside a scratch 'miniroot' with the fixture placed at the literal contracted path ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md and a copy of the real, unmodified tools/verify_lib.sh and schema/frontmatter.schema.json alongside it: GOOD passes entries 3-8 (exit 0 each); a fixture with the 'evidence' category token removed from Section 3 -- while a stray, unrelated 'evidence' word is deliberately left present in Section 7 to prove the check is section-anchored and not a vacuous whole-file grep -- correctly fails entry 5 only; four independent Section-4 mutations (dropping 'revision', 'timestamp', the literal 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions' phrase, and the literal 'FAIL condition' token, each time leaving a decoy of the missing word present elsewhere in the document) each independently and correctly fail entry 6; two independent Section-5 mutations (dropping 'immutable', dropping 'point-in-time') each independently and correctly fail entry 7; a Section-6 mutation replacing the literal `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json` path with prose correctly fails entry 8. No cross-contamination observed (each mutation fails only its targeted entry, all others still pass on the same fixture).

DEPENDENCY-ORDER GUARD (entry 14) MUTATION-PROVEN in the same scratch miniroot (schema/ file placed only in the scratch copy, never the real schema/ directory): PASSes when schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json is absent; correctly FAILs ('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json already exists -- out of dependency order or out of this feature's scope') when a stand-in file is placed at that path; returns to PASS once removed.

NON-GOAL PER-FILE EXISTENCE LOOP (entry 17) RE-CONFIRMED SOUND, NOT A REPEAT OF CONTRACT 033's ROUND-0 ls-BASED DEFECT: mutation-tested in the scratch miniroot -- PASSes on a clean tree, correctly FAILs and names the offending path when exactly one forbidden ecosystem/*.md document (04_dependency_reconciliation.md) is created, returns to PASS after removal. Entry 17 was authored using the per-file `for`/`[ -e ]` loop form from the start (as design_notes explicitly notes), not the ls-multi-argument form contract 033 had to fix in its own revision 1.

SCOPE GUARD (entry 12) AND .agent IMMUTABILITY GUARD (entry 13) RE-RUN LIVE (not merely dry-run-claimed) against the real, current working tree, which already contains feature 038's real concurrently-authored untracked .agent/contracts/038.json: both entries PASS with 038's real artifact present, confirming the concurrency-tolerance allow-lists and excludes work against a genuine concurrent artifact, not only a synthetic stand-in.

PATHSPEC-EXCLUDE NON-GOAL GUARD (entry 15) REASONED SOUND AGAINST GIT'S ACTUAL UNTRACKED-FILE SEMANTICS: confirmed via `git ls-files` that schema/README.md and every existing tools/fixtures/*.md|json|txt file are already TRACKED, so a concurrent feature-038 edit to either would show under a bare `git diff HEAD -- schema/ tools/`, making the `':(exclude)schema/README.md'` and `':(exclude)tools/fixtures'` pathspec excludes load-bearing and necessary (not decorative); brand-new untracked files 038 might add are invisible to `git diff HEAD` regardless (git diff does not surface untracked paths without --no-index or `git add -N`), so the exclude is specifically protecting against 038's legitimate edits to the two pre-existing TRACKED files it may touch, exactly as design_notes.concurrency_fence claims.

COARSE-CHECK SUPERSESSION VERIFIED LOGICALLY, NOT ASSUMED: feature_list_005.json's own coarse whole-file acceptance test for feature 034 requires the lowercased document to contain 'baseline', ('revision' or 'sha'), and ('timestamp' or 'point-in-time') anywhere. Confirmed that satisfying the strengthened, section-anchored entries 5-8 necessarily satisfies this coarse test: the literal heading text '## 3. Baseline Fields' / '## 6. Baseline Artifact' alone guarantees 'baseline' appears; entry 6 requires the literal word 'revision'; entry 6 requires 'timestamp' (entry 7 additionally requires 'point-in-time'). No coverage gap between the feature-list's original acceptance criterion and the contract's strengthened replacement.

MINOR, NON-BLOCKING CITATION IMPRECISION (not a verification-entry defect): design_notes attributes the literal artifact filename `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json` to 'NIP-0001's Artifact Locations section'; re-reading docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md's actual Artifact Locations section shows it names only the directory `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/`, not the `baseline.json` leaf filename -- the literal full path with filename actually comes from product_spec_005.md's 'Required first-run outputs' line. This is the same citation pattern already present, unchallenged, in the shipped ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Section 6 (which cites the identical NIP-0001 Artifact Locations section for its own `preflight.json` leaf filename), so it is pre-existing house convention rather than a new defect this contract introduces. It does not affect the correctness of verification entry 8, which checks the correct, spec-backed literal path string regardless of which document is credited as its source. Non-blocking; a possible future cleanup only.

MINOR, NON-BLOCKING, LOW-RISK OBSERVATION: verification entry 5's per-token loop checks the bare abbreviation 'CI' (case-sensitive substring within the Section 3 span) rather than a longer, less coincidentally-matchable phrase. No English word in ordinary prose contains the literal uppercase two-letter substring 'CI', so the realistic false-positive risk is negligible; still flagged for completeness since no identical single-token abbreviation check exists in prior contracts 031/032/033 to point to as precedent.

REPOSITORY LEFT BYTE-FOR-BYTE UNMODIFIED BY THIS REVIEW'S OWN ACTIVITY: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` and `git diff HEAD --stat` were checked immediately before and after all probing and are identical throughout (only the three pre-existing untracked files .agent/contracts/034.json, .agent/contracts/038.json, and v0.6.0-release-notes.md, none created or touched by this review). ALL fixtures, the disposable git clone, and the scratch 'miniroot' were created and read exclusively under the evaluator's own scratchpad directory; no path under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, or any .agent path other than this review's own .agent/qa/034-contract-review.json was created, modified, or deleted. Feature 038's real, concurrently in-flight surfaces (.agent/contracts/038.json; schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, tools/fixtures/* not yet landed) were observed only, never touched or simulated at their real paths.

No new defect, vacuous check, false-pass, false-fail, or unsupported claim was found in revision 1. The round-0 finding (verification entry 9's backslash-in-single-quote defect) is independently confirmed closed with the evaluator's own fresh, reproducible probe in a disposable clone, not merely trusted from the generator's design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof claim.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. The contract is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json. All 18 verification entries were extracted programmatically from the live contract (never retyped) and re-run from the repository root against the current, unimplemented tree: entries 1/3/4/5/6/7/8/9/10 correctly FAIL (target artifact absent, README not yet flipped) and entries 2/11/12/13/14/15/16/17/18 correctly PASS, exactly matching design_notes' claimed pattern. The round-0 backslash-backtick defect in entry 9 is independently re-confirmed fixed, character-exact, using the evaluator's own probe in a disposable local git clone (never the real repository): the corrected plain-backtick pattern PASSes against a genuinely, correctly flipped README and FAILs against the unmodified one. Entries 9 and 10 were further stress-tested against the exact gaming vectors the task specified: a wrong-row flip (00 instead of 02) is caught by entry 9 alone; a two-row flip (both 00 and 02) is caught by entry 10 alone; no mutation was found that passes both entries without the 02 row being the sole, correct edit. The section-anchored content checks (entries 5-8) were independently fixture- and mutation-tested against an evaluator-built GOOD document and eight targeted BAD mutations (one per required token/phrase, each with a decoy of the missing term deliberately left elsewhere in the document to prove section-anchoring, not vacuous whole-file matching) run via the unmodified verification commands in a scratch 'miniroot' containing the real, unmodified tools/verify_lib.sh and schema/frontmatter.schema.json: every mutation fails exactly and only its targeted entry. The dependency-order guard (entry 14) and the non-goal per-file existence loop (entry 17, correctly using the per-file form from the start, not a repeat of contract 033's round-0 ls-based defect) were each mutation-tested and correctly discriminate. The scope guard and .agent immutability guard (entries 12, 13) were re-run against the real working tree, which already contains feature 038's genuine concurrent .agent/contracts/038.json, and correctly tolerate it. The pathspec-exclude non-goal guard (entry 15) was reasoned sound against git's actual untracked-file diff semantics (schema/README.md and existing tools/fixtures/* entries are tracked, making the excludes load-bearing; new untracked files 038 might add are invisible to `git diff HEAD` regardless). Two minor, non-blocking observations were logged: a citation imprecision in design_notes attributing a literal filename to NIP-0001's Artifact Locations section (pre-existing house convention, does not affect any check's correctness) and a low-risk single-token 'CI' abbreviation match with no realistic false-positive vector. The repository was left byte-for-byte unmodified by this review's own activity; all probes, fixtures, and the disposable clone live exclusively under the evaluator's own scratchpad, and feature 038's real concurrent surfaces were observed only, never touched or simulated at their real paths. Fit for implementation as-is; cleared to advance to Loop-2.
